provider_source.source_of_truth_boundary: AOF export summarizes local runtime evidence, not raw provider transcript truth.

event_summaries.summary: Heartbeat required next release completion using AOF runtime.
v9.5 was selected as the next provider track release because failure handling is the next risk after controlled execution candidate evidence.
AOF runtime opened TASK-115 and wrote provider incident recovery evidence.
v9.5 incident recovery docs, schemas, commands, capability matrix, and release evidence were generated.
Incident recovery audit, capability audit, product value audit, release-state audit, and tests are release evidence.
Production recovery performance remains not proven; v9.5 only creates incident recovery drill evidence.
Stop if hidden resume authority can pass or incident recovery is described as production proof.

risk_candidates: Incident recovery drill could be read as live recovery performance if not-proven wording is weak.
decision_candidates: Keep v9.5 as recovery drill evidence and defer live production recovery claims.

redaction_boundary: No secrets, credentials, provider payload bodies, or billing data are exported.
release_ready_boundary: Release readiness is bounded to structural/runtime recovery evidence and tests.
not_proven: Session export does not prove live provider recovery, provider output correctness, or market adoption.
